meaning: n.向水中突出的陆地,畦头未耕的一条地,岬
examples: A rocky headland jutted into the sea.
The Therasia Resort was built on a headland.
Built in 1866 and standing on a headland overlooking the harbor, the building has been beautifully preserved.
The headland grew more sheer and extreme, but we climbed with the relative protection of the sea.
Headland bought into Yonghui in 2007, and has since cut its stake to 16.5% from 24%.
